region_of_origin: Malay Peninsula — Singapore, Malaysia, and Indonesia

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Serunding Kelapa. Closest parent is spiced desiccated coconut condiment. No exact FoodOn URI assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Spiced dry-fried desiccated coconut condiment (Malay serunding kelapa style)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A01MN is the closest defensible FoodEx2 parent for a dry-fried spiced coconut condiment; no exact code exists for serunding kelapa.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for serunding kelapa as a specific food entity.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of serunding kelapa as a condiment is approximately 2 tablespoons (20g), sprinkled over nasi lemak, lontong, or ketupat.

nutrient_source.title: Serunding Kelapa — IMR Malaysia MyFCD (Malaysian Food Composition Database)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the IMR Malaysia MyFCD entry for Serunding Kelapa. Copper, manganese, and vitamin K values supplemented from enriched_json with confidence 0.9 where IMR data was not available for those micronutrients.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index estimation for serunding kelapa based on analogous high-fat, high-fibre desiccated coconut products
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for serunding kelapa in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 40 (low) is estimated by analogy with high-fat, high-fibre desiccated coconut products. The very high fat content (38.5g/100g, predominantly medium-chain saturated fatty acids from coconut) and high dietary fibre (8.0g/100g) substantially attenuate postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load per 20g serving = (40 × 2.5g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 1.0, confirming negligible glycaemic impact at condiment serving sizes.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 420mg/100g; very high saturated fat (33.5g/100g) raises secondary cardiovascular concern
hypertension.guidance: A standard 20g condiment serving delivers approximately 84mg sodium, which is manageable within a 2000mg daily sodium limit; however, patients with hypertension should limit serunding to no more than 20g per meal and be aware that the high saturated fat content poses an independent cardiovascular risk that compounds hypertensive disease.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (GI 40) and very low glycemic load per serving (GL 1.0) due to high fat and fibre content
type2_diabetes.guidance: A 20g serving of serunding kelapa delivers only 2.5g available carbohydrate with a glycemic load of approximately 1.0, making it safe from a glycaemic perspective for people with type 2 diabetes when used as a condiment; total caloric density (398 kcal/100g) should be considered in the context of overall energy management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Extremely high saturated fat content (33.5g/100g, predominantly lauric acid from desiccated coconut)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Serunding kelapa is very high in saturated fat (33.5g/100g), predominantly medium-chain lauric acid from coconut; while lauric acid has a less atherogenic profile than long-chain saturates, patients with established cardiovascular disease should restrict intake to no more than 1 teaspoon (5g) per meal to limit saturated fat contribution to under 1.7g per serving.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High aggregate saturated fat intake may promote systemic inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should limit serunding kelapa to 1 teaspoon (5g) per meal; the iron content (2.8mg/100g) is beneficial for anaemia of chronic inflammation, and the anti-inflammatory spices (turmeric, galangal) provide modest benefit, but these do not offset the pro-inflammatory risk of high saturated fat at large serving sizes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Serunding Kelapa is a fragrant, intensely flavoured dry coconut condiment traditional to Malay cuisine in Singapore, Malaysia, and Indonesia. It is made by slowly dry-frying freshly grated coconut in a wok together with a spice paste of shallots, garlic, lemongrass, galangal, turmeric, and dried chillies, seasoned with palm sugar, tamarind, and salt, until the coconut becomes completely moisture-free, golden-brown, crumbly, and floss-like in texture. The result is a savoury-sweet-spiced coconut floss with a rich, nutty, aromatic flavour and a dry, slightly crispy texture. Nutritionally, serunding kelapa is very high in saturated fat (33.5g/100g from desiccated coconut, predominantly medium-chain lauric acid) and energy-dense (398 kcal/100g), but the realistic condiment portion of 20g limits actual fat intake to approximately 7.7g saturated fat per serving. It has a low glycemic index (estimated GI 40) and a negligible glycemic load per serving (GL 1.0) due to its high fat and fibre content (8.0g/100g dietary fibre). Sodium is 420mg/100g, yielding 84mg per 20g serving. It is served as an accompaniment to lontong, ketupat, nasi lemak, and nasi ambeng, and is a traditional Hari Raya condiment. It is suitable for people with type 2 diabetes at condiment portions but should be used sparingly by those with cardiovascular disease or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high saturated fat content.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced from IMR Malaysia MyFCD (exact match for serunding kelapa). GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database methodology applied to high-fat, high-fibre desiccated coconut products. Minor micronutrients (copper, manganese, vitamin K) supplemented from enriched_json at confidence 0.9. Overall record confidence is high given the primary regional source match.
notes: Trans fat (0.0g) and cholesterol (0.0g) are confirmed zero per IMR Malaysia MyFCD — serunding kelapa is a plant-based coconut product with no animal-derived ingredients and no industrial hydrogenation. Vitamin B12 (0.0g) is naturally zero as this is a fully plant-based food with no animal-derived components. Vitamin D (0.0g) is naturally zero as coconut and spice ingredients contain no significant vitamin D. Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat breakdown not reported in IMR source; set to -1. Water content not reported; set to -1. Two portions are noted in the input: 15g (enriched_json) and 20g (verified); the 20g verified portion is used as the primary serving for GL calculation.
